general: RMF: Max deduction up to 30% of taxable income, capped at 500k Baht.
ThaiESG: Max deduction up to 30% of taxable income, capped at 300k Baht.
Solar Rooftop 2569: New deduction up to 200k Baht for on-grid home solar installation.
Combine RMF + Provident Fund + Pension Insurance capped at 500k Baht total.
ThaiESG has its own separate limit of 300k Baht (not in the 500k cap)!
Thai Tax 2569: Thai ESG วงเงิน 300k สุดท้าย — ลดเหลือ 100k ปี 2570!
SSF: Purchases are discontinued starting 2025. Existing holdings must still be held for 10 full years.
ETF: Buy and sell directly on the stock exchange during trading hours. No holding lockups apply.
e-Donation: Double deduction (200%) for educational and hospital donations, verified via EDOC.
Solar Rooftop: Save on electricity and get up to 200k Baht tax deduction for home installations in 2569.
Provident Fund (PVD): Employers match contributions, giving you instant return before tax savings.
Social Security: Deduct up to 9,000 Baht per year based on monthly contributions.
Life Insurance: Deduct premiums up to 100k Baht. Must have at least 10-year maturity period.
Health Insurance: Deduct premiums up to 25k Baht. Combined with general life insurance capped at 100k Baht.
Parent Care: Deduct 30k Baht per dependent parent aged 60+ earning less than 30k Baht annually.

rmf: RMF: Max deduction up to 30% of taxable income, capped at 500k Baht (combined with other retirement funds).
RMF: Must invest at least every other year and hold until age 55 & at least 5 years to keep tax status.
RMF: Switches between RMF funds are tax-free if transferred directly. Do not withdraw early!
RMF: No dividends are paid. All gains are automatically reinvested for maximum long-term compound growth.
RMF: Outstanding for long-term target planning since they force savings discipline until age 55.
esg: ThaiESG: Max deduction up to 30% of taxable income, capped at 300k Baht (separate from the 500k retirement cap).
ThaiESG: Holding period is 5 full years (purchased date-to-date). No minimum annual investment required.
ThaiESG 2569: The 300k limit is active for 2569; it is set to decrease to 100k in 2570!
ThaiESG: Focuses on sustainability, local governance, and carbon-reduction projects in Thailand.
ThaiESG: Excellent choice for high-income earners who have already maxed out their 500k retirement cap.
esgx: ESGX: Part of the ThaiESG 300k Baht separate cap. Focuses on high-impact environmental projects.
ESGX: Typically features shorter holding structures or extra incentives. Validate exact terms before buying.
ESGX: Tracks companies with prime sustainability ratings and active carbon-neutral transitions.
ESGX: A premium sub-class aimed at focused ESG/sustainability transition leaders.
ssf: SSF: Discontinued for new purchases starting Jan 2025. Old purchases must still fulfill the 10-year lockup!
SSF: Holding period is 10 years *date-to-date* (from the exact purchase date).
SSF: Max deduction was up to 30% of taxable income, capped at 200k Baht (part of the 500k retirement cap).
SSF: No continuity rule. You can skip years without losing privileges on previous investments.
etf: ETF: No tax deduction benefits, but tradeable in real-time on the Stock Exchange of Thailand (SET).
ETF: Offers lower management fees than traditional mutual funds. Great for passive index tracking.
ETF: Highly liquid and pays regular dividends depending on the underlying fund policy.
ETF: Trades using real-time bidding spreads just like single shares during market hours.
